{{Quote|Being a Jedi is not just about ''power'', or lightsabers, or even skill with the Force. It is about ''connection''. Being part of something bigger. I am stronger as part of the Jedi Order than I could ever be alone.|Obi-Wan Kenobi, to Anakin Skywalker|<ref name="Obi-Wan and Anakin 5">[[Obi-Wan and Anakin 5|''Obi-Wan and Anakin'' 5]]</ref>}}
The '''Jedi Order''', also known as the '''Order of the Jedi''', and the '''New Jedi Order''' under [[Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker]], was a noble monastic and nontheistic [[Religion|religious]] order united in their devotion to the [[light side of the Force]]. They served as mediators, warriors, and with teaching and exploratory efforts. With a [[Timeline of galactic history|history]] dating back [[Millennium|thousands of years]] before the [[Imperial Era|rise]] of the [[Galactic Empire]], the [[Jedi Knight]]s—noted for their [[lightsaber]]s and [[Midi-chlorian|natural]] [[Force-sensitive|ability]] to harness the [[Force power|powers]] of [[the Force]]—stood as the [[Law enforcement agency|guardians of peace and justice]] in the [[Galactic Republic]]. The Order was originally established on [[Ahch-To]] in the [[Unknown Regions]]. In later years, the [[Imperial Palace|Jedi Grand Temple]] on the [[Core Worlds|Core World]] [[Coruscant]] served as the hub of all [[Jedi]] activity in [[the galaxy]]. The [[Jedi temple|Temple]] was also a [[Jedi training|training]] school for [[Jedi Initiate|younglings]] and [[Padawan]]s, who learned the ways of the Force under the supervision of Jedi Masters, twelve of whom were elected to serve on the [[Jedi High Council]]—the Order's highest governing authority. The High [[Jedi Council|Council's]] staff [[officer]]s were the [[Grand Master]], who functioned as the leader of the Order and was a title reserved for the wisest Jedi Master, and the [[Master of the Order]], who served as the Council's appointed leader. The [[Jedi Code]] governed the Order's way of life; therefore, every Jedi was duty-bound to observe and uphold the Code or else risk expulsion from the Order.

The emergence of the [[Sith]] Order was the result of a [[Hundred-Year Darkness|schism]] that fractured Jedi unity, dividing the Order between the Jedi who remained faithful to the light and a [[Jedi splinter group|group of renegades]] that followed a [[Unidentified rogue Jedi|rogue Jedi]] into exile for exploring the [[dark side of the Force]]. Thenceforth, the Jedi and Sith [[Sith war (Old Republic)|warred]] with each other for [[Standard century|centuries]]. Although the [[Jedi-Sith War|last conflict]] concluded with the [[fall of the Old Republic]], the ancient Sith all but perished as well, allowing a new [[Galaxy|Galactic]] Republic to rise under the protection of the Jedi Order. Unknown to the Jedi, however, the Sith endured in [[Grand Plan|secret]] for a millennium beginning with [[Darth Bane]], the sole-surviving [[Dark Lord of the Sith]] who reformed the [[Code of the Sith|Sith way]] by creating the [[Rule of Two]]—a rule limiting the Sith Order's ranks to two members: a [[Sith Master]] to embody the power of the dark side, and a [[Sith apprentice]] to covet that power. While the Jedi and Republic moved on, their ancient wars with the Sith fading from memory, Bane's legacy spawned a lineage of [[Sith Lord]]s that culminated in the rise of [[Darth Sidious]] approximately [[32 BBY|three decades]] before the [[Battle of Yavin]].

During the [[Republic Era|final decades]] of the Republic, the Jedi Order vowed allegiance to the [[Galactic Senate]] as the galaxy was beset by [[Politics|political]] instability and social unrest which, in turn, created a [[Separatist Crisis|secessionist crisis]] that ultimately resulted in the formation of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. Lacking the sufficient manpower needed to protect the galaxy, the Jedi became [[Jedi Commander|commanders]] and [[Jedi General|generals]] in the newly-formed [[Grand Army of the Republic]]. For roughly three years they commanded [[Army|armies]] of [[clone trooper]]s in a conflict known as the [[Clone Wars]], engaging the Confederacy's [[battle droid]] [[Confederacy military|forces]] led by [[Count]] [[Dooku]]—a former Jedi Master who became a Sith [[Lord]]—and the [[Cyborg|cybernetic]] [[warlord]] [[General]] [[Grievous]]. Though the Jedi fought to preserve the Republic, the government they served was secretly taken over by Sidious who, as [[Senator]] Sheev Palpatine of [[Naboo]], rose to power in the Senate and became the last [[Supreme Chancellor]] of the Republic. The Jedi were unknowingly being deceived by the Sith.

The Chancellor's deceptions and machinations culminated in the execution of [[Order 66]], a [[Executive Order Codes for the Grand Army of the Republic|secret protocol]] that [[Behavioral modification biochip|forcibly turned]] the Jedi's [[Cloning|clone]] [[soldier]]s against them, leading to an all but total [[Great Jedi Purge|extermination]] of the Jedi Order. With most of the Jedi eliminated, Sidious [[Proclamation of the New Order|supplanted]] a thousand years of [[democracy]] with a [[New Order|new government]] known as the Galactic Empire. In addition, Sidious succeeded in convincing [[Anakin Skywalker]]—the prophesied [[Chosen One]] who was destined to bring balance to the Force by destroying the Sith—to betray the Jedi and join the dark side, with Sidious renaming him Darth Vader. As a Sith Lord, the former Jedi Knight served the self-proclaimed [[Galactic Emperor]] by hunting down the few surviving Jedi, who had gone into hiding across the galaxy. In spite of the Sith's efforts, however, the Jedi continued to endure. Within a generation of the Empire's rise to power, [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Yoda]]—both of whom survived the initial purge of the Order—trained Vader's son, Luke Skywalker, in the Jedi arts. Luke would later redeem his father, causing the redeemed Anakin Skywalker to return to the light and fulfill his destiny as the Chosen One, [[Death|destroying]] both himself and his Master during the [[Battle of Endor]].

The Empire [[Galactic Concordance|dissolved]] into various remnants as a result of their [[Battle of Jakku|defeat]] in the [[Galactic Civil War]], allowing the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic]] to rise as the [[New Republic]]. Under the [[New Republic Era|new era]], Luke Skywalker endeavored to rebuild the Order by training a new generation of Jedi. Among Skywalker's students was his nephew, [[Ben Solo]], the son of his [[twin]] sister, [[Princess]] [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Organa]], and the famed [[smuggler]] [[Han Solo]]. Ben, however, was seduced by the dark side of the Force at the coaxing of [[Snoke]], an enigmatic figure who spearheaded the Imperial-inspired [[First Order]]. Luke confronted and frightened Ben, who fought back and fled. As a result, Ben adopted the name and persona of Kylo Ren, a First Order [[warlord]] and [[Ren|master]] of the [[Knights of Ren]].

The new generation was betrayed and [[Destruction of the Jedi Temple|destroyed]] by Ren, who left the [[Jedi Temple of Luke Skywalker|Jedi training temple]] [[Fire|burning]] by the [[time]] Skywalker recovered from the attack. Feeling responsible for Ben's fall and the massacre of his apprentices, he went into exile and disappeared from the galaxy, leaving only traces of his location on a [[star]] [[Map to Skywalker|map]]. The First Order and the [[Resistance]] both sought to acquire the map, but ultimately it was the latter that succeeded due to the actions of the [[Jakku]] [[scavenger]] [[Rey Skywalker|Rey]]. She followed the map to the [[planet]] [[Ahch-To]], where the last Jedi had taken [[Hermit|refuge]] in the [[first Jedi Temple]].

Skywalker, stricken by grief and believing his return would only cause more problems, was reluctant to train Rey or another generation of Jedi. However, the last Jedi eventually returned to the fight, buying time for the Resistance to escape the [[Battle of Crait|siege of Crait]] led by Kylo Ren, who became the new [[Supreme Leader]] after [[Battle of Oetchi|killing his master]], Snoke. Skywalker became [[Force spirit|one with the Force]] after exerting a great amount of energy to [[Force projection|project]] himself across the galaxy to face his fallen nephew, but not before acknowledging that he would not be the last Jedi. His heroic tale spread across the galaxy, inspiring others and giving hope in a [[First Order-Resistance War|time of rising darkness]].

Having returned from death, Darth Sidious urged Kylo Ren to end the Jedi Order by killing Rey, who was his [[Palpatine family|granddaughter]]. After Ren renounced the dark side, Sidious [[Battle of Exegol|attempted to turn]] Rey until he discovered her [[Force-bond|bond]] with Ben Solo. Realizing they were a [[Force dyad|dyad in the Force]], Sidious nearly drained them of their life force to regain his full power. Though weakened by Sidious’ attack, Rey was strengthened by the spirits of past Jedi, allowing the apprentice to stand against the Dark Lord of the Sith. She destroyed her grandfather using his own [[Force lightning]] against him, preventing the rebirth of the Sith Order, but the effort left her dead. Solo [[Force healing|resurrected]] her at the cost of his life, becoming one with the Force as the last Skywalker. The Skywalkers were gone, but their legacy endured through Rey, who took the Skywalker name to honor her mentors and restore the Jedi Order.

==Description==
===Overview===
{{Quote|The Order tends to be what it needs to be for any particular time. Sometimes, we're warriors, fighting the forces of evil. Sometimes, we govern. Sometimes, we do the opposite—drawing inward, studying the Force and its mysteries. Sometimes, we explore. Sometimes, we do all of those things. And sometimes… We are carved down to almost nothing. Those who are left must bear the responsibility of being one last bit of light in a galaxy filled with endless dark.|Elzar Mann, to Luke Skywalker|<ref name="Star Wars (2020) 20">[[Star Wars (2020) 20|''Star Wars'' (2020) 20]]</ref>}}
[[File:The_High_Republic_The_Fallen_Star_unlettered_cover.jpg|thumb|left|250px|For a thousand generations, the Jedi Order protected the Galactic Republic as guardians of peace and justice.]]
The [[Jedi Knight]]s<ref name="Episode IV">{{Film|IV}}</ref> were a noble order of [[Force-sensitive]] protectors—united by their ability to use [[the Force]]—who upheld peace and justice in the [[Galactic Republic]] for over a thousand generations,<ref name="DB:Order" /> carrying out what they sensed to be the will of the Force as its instruments.<ref name="Light of the Jedi" /> As [[Law enforcement agency|keepers of the peace]], the [[Jedi]] Order<ref name="UltimateSW" />—also referred to as the Order of the Jedi<ref name="Day">{{Acolyte|Day (episode)}}</ref>—undertook [[Politics|diplomatic]] missions on behalf of the [[Galactic Senate]], and pursued [[Planet|interplanetary]] criminals across [[the galaxy]].<ref name="UltimateSW" /> Whereas the [[Sith]]—the [[Timeline of galactic history|historic]] enemies of the Jedi Order—harnessed the [[dark side of the Force]] for power,<ref name="DB:Sith">{{Databank|sith|Sith}}</ref> the Jedi followed the ways of the [[light side of the Force]].<ref name="DB:Order" /> The [[Jedi Code]],<ref name="TSWB" /> along with a collection of [[Sacred Jedi texts|other foundational texts]],<ref name="Episode VIII" /> guided the Jedi way of life, governing the behavior of individual Jedi to the philosophy and [[Jedi training|training]] regimen of the Order as a whole.<ref name="TSWB" /> Though the Jedi worshipped no specific [[deity]], the [[Chosen One]] played an important role in Jedi prophecy and mythology.<ref name="Master & Apprentice">''[[Master & Apprentice]]''</ref> According to [[Padawan]] [[Matthea Cathley]], the Jedi worked better as a group instead of on their own;<ref name="Path of Vengeance" /> indeed, Jedi Knight [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] once reflected that, as part of the Order, he was stronger than if he was on his own.<ref name="Obi-Wan and Anakin 5" />

The virtues of the Order included compassion, generosity, peaceful resolution,<ref name="Catalyst">''[[Catalyst: A Rogue One Novel]]''</ref> and spreading justice.<ref name="Convergence">''[[The High Republic: Convergence]]''</ref> As spelled out directly in one version of the Jedi Code, a Jedi was not supposed to act in pursuit of personal power or wealth, instead acting only with the goals of finding knowledge and enlightenment.<ref name="Path of Vengeance">''[[The High Republic: Path of Vengeance]]''</ref> Further Jedi goals included finding balance<ref name="Age Qui-Gon">[[Age of Republic - Qui-Gon Jinn 1|''Age of Republic - Qui-Gon Jinn'' 1]]</ref> and protecting others in a just fashion<ref name="Convergence" /> as part of a selfless quest to aid their fellow beings.<ref name="Episode III" /> Nonetheless, Jedi needed to understand they were, like all people, imperfect and needed to always learn to be better.<ref name="Convergence" /> Like anyone, a Jedi could also be scared, but a Jedi was supposed to not run from their fears<ref name="Path of Vengeance" /> or let those fears consume them,<ref name="Episode III" /> instead facing those fears directly and remaining confident in the knowledge the Force was with them.<ref name="Path of Vengeance" /> In short, their duties as servants of light and the Republic could be summed up as teachers, explorers, diplomats, and warriors.<ref name="Light of the Jedi" />

The Jedi were instructed in the ways of the Order and the Force; as their knowledge grew, each member progressed through several different ranks. With the exception of [[Anakin Skywalker]], prospective Jedi were identified a short [[time]] after [[Reproduction|birth]] by the quantity of [[midi-chlorian]]s in their [[blood]], a factor which indicated the level of a subject's Force-sensitivity.<ref name="Episode I" /> Many of these children were listed within Jedi [[holocron]]s before being identified by the Order. Training began at the level of the [[Jedi Initiate]]; at some point in their training, [[youngling]]s were expected to find their own [[Kyber crystal]]—the heart of the [[lightsaber]],<ref name="DB:Kyber">{{Databank|lightsaber-crystal|Kyber Crystal (Lightsaber Crystal)}}</ref> a distinctive and elegant weapon of the Jedi<ref name="DB:Lightsaber">{{Databank|lightsaber|Lightsaber}}</ref>—in order to pass an ancient rite of passage known as [[Gathering|The Gathering]].<ref name="DB:Ilum">{{Databank|ilum|Ilum}}</ref>

[[File:Coruscantsunset.png|thumb|right|250px|Padawans were assigned to fully-trained Jedi, such as Knights or Masters, in order to complete their training in the Jedi arts.]]
Younglings who succeeded in their trials graduated to the level of Padawan, the apprentice of a more experienced member of the Order. In accordance with the Jedi Code, a Master could instruct only one student in the ways of the Force at any given time. The [[Padawan braid]], a symbol that represented a Jedi apprentice's status in the Order, was worn in the [[hair]]<ref name="Episode I" /> or head,<ref name="TCWFilm">[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (film)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' film]]</ref> depending on the Padawan's [[species]].<ref name="Episode I" /><ref name="TWJ">{{TCW|The Wrong Jedi}}</ref> The braid was removed when a Padawan ascended to the rank of Jedi Knight or in the case of [[Exile (punishment)|banishment]] from the Order's ranks.<ref name="Wrong Jedi Ep Gallery">{{SW|url=series/clone-wars/the-wrong-jedi-episode-gallery|text="The Wrong Jedi" Episode Gallery}}</ref>

The [[Jedi Trials]] were a series of tests that ultimately determined whether a Padawan was worthy of the rank of Jedi Knight.<ref name="Episode I" /> After becoming a full-fledged member of the Order, a Jedi could choose to adopt a Padawan of their own as part of the Order's tradition in training the next generation of Jedi.<ref name="TCWFilm" /> Teaching the ways of the Force to the next generation of Jedi was considered one of the greatest honors in the Order, its existence and traditions perpetuated through "a cycle of training and education."<ref name="TSWB" /> The [[Eye of the Nihil]], [[Marchion Ro]], believed that being structured into an order gave Jedi an advantage; in addition to tapping into the Force, any Jedi could tap into their Order to find confidence and structure. Being part of a united group made it so individual Jedi were willing to make large–scale decisions, which they believed would spread their light throughout the galaxy. In effect, a Jedi was not merely strong and bold because they could use the Force. Instead, being part of the overall Jedi Order helped them grow to embody those traits.<ref name="Light of the Jedi" />

[[File:Jedicouncil2.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Comprised of twelve Jedi Masters, the Jedi High Council was the governing body of the Jedi Order.]]
The most powerful and legendary Jedi held the rank of Jedi Master<ref name="DB:Yoda">{{Databank|yoda|Yoda}}</ref> once they had proven themself worthy of the independence<ref name="Light of the Jedi" /> and station granted by the title. Again with the exception of Skywalker, the [[Jedi High Council]]<ref name="Episode III" />—the highest governing body of the Order<ref name="UltimateSW" />—consisted of Masters throughout the history of the Order.<ref name="Episode III" /> Among the High Council, the title of [[Grand Master]] was conferred on the oldest and wisest member of their group,<ref name="UFC">''[[Ultimate Factivity Collection: Star Wars]]''</ref> while the Council's leadership rested with the [[Master of the Order]].<ref name="Rise of the Separatists" />

The Jedi Order in its capacity as a peacekeeping force of the Republic<ref name="Episode II" /> vowed allegiance to the Galactic Senate and [[democracy]].<ref name="Episode III" /> As an "extension" of the Republic's [[Judicial Department|Judiciary branch]],<ref name="TSWB" /> the Order was under the legal jurisdiction of the Senate,<ref name="Obi-Wan & Anakin 1">[[Obi-Wan and Anakin 1|''Obi-Wan and Anakin'' 1]]</ref> and as leader of the Senate,<ref name="Episode III" /> the [[Supreme Chancellor]] had the privilege to make requests of the Order.<ref name="Obi-Wan & Anakin 1" /> However, this prerogative did not give the chancellor direct authority over the Order, which was legally under senatorial oversight. According to [[Darth Sidious|Sheev Palpatine]], the [[Office of the Chancellor]] would become a [[dictatorship]] if the Order was forced to comply with orders from the Republic head of state.<ref name="Obi-Wan & Anakin 4">[[Obi-Wan and Anakin 4|''Obi-Wan and Anakin'' 4]]</ref>

===Locations===
{{Quote|The most famous of the Jedi temples, located on Coruscant, served as the home of the Jedi High Council and the vast library of data known as the Jedi Archives.|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="Secrets of the Jedi">''[[Star Wars: The Secrets of the Jedi]]''</ref>}}
[[File:First_Jedi_Temple_TLJ.png|thumb|right|250px|The first Jedi Temple was built on a sacred island of Ahch-To.]]
[[Jedi temple]]s served as places of worship, training schools, and repositories of knowledge for the Jedi Order.<ref name="Secrets of the Jedi" /> The [[first Jedi Temple]] was built atop a [[Temple Island|sacred island]] on [[Ahch-To]]<ref name="Ahch-To Jedi Temple Databank">{{Databank|ahch-to-jedi-temple|Ahch-To Jedi Temple}}</ref> tens of thousands of years before the [[First Order-Resistance War]].<ref name="Constructed date">''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary]]'' states that the first [[Jedi temple|Jedi Temple]] was constructed on Ahch-To tens of thousands of [[Standard year|years]] before the events of [[Star Wars: Episode VIII The Last Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VIII ''The Last Jedi'']]. ''Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary'' places ''Star Wars'': Episode VIII ''The Last Jedi'' in the same year as the events of [[Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens|''Star Wars'': Episode VII ''The Force Awakens'']], which ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' dates to [[34 ABY]]. Therefore, the first Jedi Temple was constructed tens of thousands of years before 34 ABY.</ref> The temple rose high above the surrounding sea, and was worn down by erosion and time. Some signs of its past remained, however, such as mosaics and an ancient [[meditation]] ledge.<ref name="Ahch-To Jedi Temple Databank" /> One such mosaic depicted the Prime Jedi in a state of meditation; the same mosaic symbolized the nature of the Force as a duality between the dark side and the light.<ref name="TROS VD">''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref>

[[File:JediTemple_cloudysky.jpg|thumb|left|250px|During the era of the Galactic Republic, the Jedi Order made its home in the Jedi Grand Temple on Coruscant.]]
The Jedi Order made its home in the [[Imperial Palace|Jedi Grand Temple]] on [[Coruscant]],<ref name="DB:JediTemple" /> the [[capital]] world of the Galactic Republic.<ref name="DB:Coruscant">{{Databank|coruscant|Coruscant}}</ref> Over the millennia, the Temple served as the central hub of all Jedi activities in the galaxy; it was a school, a monastery, and the headquarters of the Jedi High Council.<ref name="DB:JediTemple" /> As a school, the Temple was the largest active Jedi training center in the galaxy<ref name="UltimateSW" /> where younglings trained to become Padawans and ultimately full-fledged Jedi Knights. The [[Jedi Archives]], the source of all knowledge accumulated by the Order, was located inside of the Temple.<ref name="Locations Gallery">{{Databank|url=jedi-temple-location-gallery|title=Jedi Temple Location Gallery}}</ref>

As the symbol of the Order, the Temple signified the Order's status in galactic affairs.<ref name="TCaJ">{{TCW|To Catch a Jedi}}</ref> While access to the Temple was not restricted to just the Jedi, all non-Jedi personnel were required to undergo compulsory entry tests before becoming employed at the Temple. Those who worked in the Temple often dedicated their whole life to serving the Order.<ref name="Sabotage">{{TCW|Sabotage (episode)}}</ref> Having sustained extensive damage during the fall of the Jedi, the Temple was renovated and renamed as the Imperial Palace,<ref name="DB:JediTemple" /> with only the original structure's corpse and five skyscraping towers remaining intact.<ref name="Tarkin">[[Tarkin (novel)|''Tarkin'']]</ref> In addition to the Temple on Coruscant, the Order maintained several ancient temples spread across the galaxy.<ref name="PotJ">{{Rebels|Path of the Jedi}}</ref> Jedi understood they were connected to the entire galaxy through their Order, seeing the galaxy itself as their home instead of a singular location as deserving that term.<ref name="Convergence" /> 

[[File:Jedi_Temple_Ilum.png|thumb|right|250px|The Jedi Temple on Ilum was used for a sacred ritual known as The Gathering.]]
One [[Lothal Jedi Temple|ancient temple]] was located on [[Lothal]], and was only accessible if both the Master and Padawan were present. The Lothal temple was where Padawans were sent to confront their fear without the support of the Masters, some of whom ultimately died while waiting for their students who never returned, leaving the temple's antechambers littered with their skeletal remains.<ref name="PotJ" /> Other Jedi enclaves included the [[Temple of Eedit]] on [[Devaron]],<ref name="Monster">{{TCW|Monster}}</ref><ref name="weapon-jedi">''[[The Weapon of a Jedi: A Luke Skywalker Adventure]]''</ref> the [[Jedi Temple (Vrogas Vas)|Temple]] of [[Vrogas Vas]],<ref name="SW9">[[Star Wars (2015) 9|''Star Wars'' (2015) 9]]</ref> and the [[Jedi Temple (Ilum)|Temple]] of [[Ilum]] where younglings were sent to participate in The Gathering.<ref name="DB:Ilum" /> The Jedi Order established smaller outposts at various locations around the galaxy, including [[Elphrona Outpost|one]] on [[Elphrona]]. These outposts dated as far back as the High Republic Era and the great Jedi spread across the galaxy that saw them work alongside various communities. It contained various artifacts that were important to the history of the Jedi.<ref name="Rise of Kylo Ren 2">[[The Rise of Kylo Ren 2|''The Rise of Kylo Ren'' 2]]</ref>

While Ilum was regarded as the most sacred planet to the Jedi Order,<ref name="DB:Ilum" /> the [[desert]] [[moon]] [[Jedha]]—a holy site and home to one of the first civilizations that explored the nature of the Force<ref name="DB:Jedha">{{Databank|jedha|Jedha}}</ref>—was considered a spiritual home of the Jedi.<ref name="SlashFilm Jedha">{{WebCite|url=http://www.slashfilm.com/gareth-edwards-rogue-one-interview|text=Video Interview With 'Rogue One: A Star Wars Story' Director Gareth Edwards|author=Sciretta, Peter|date=2016-07-15|work=SlashFilm|archivedate=20160716124713}}</ref> Like Ilum,<ref name="DB:Ilum" /> Jedha contained the kyber [[crystal]]s that the Jedi used to create their lightsaber.<ref name="Jedha LS">{{Twitter|extratv|status/754636808714088448|[[Wikipedia:Extra (TV program)|ExtraTV]]|quote=[[Donnie Yen|@DonnieYenCT]] reveals connection between planet Jedha & lightsabers! #RogueOne #StarWars #SWCE|archivedate=20161130161620}}</ref> It was also a holy site for pilgrims throughout the galaxy, who sought spiritual guidance<ref name="DB:Jedha" /> in the [[Jedha City|Holy City]] of Jedha.<ref name="DB:Holy City">{{Databank|holy-city-of-jedha|Holy City of Jedha}}</ref> At one time it was an important world to the Jedi.<ref name="DB:Jedha" />

[[File:Luke_Skywalkers_Jedi_temple.png|thumb|left|250px|The Jedi Temple on Ossus served as a training school for the apprentices of Jedi Master Luke Skywalker.]]
A number of Jedi sites could also be found on the planets [[Tython]] and [[Ossus]], such as the [[Jedi Temple (Tython)|Jedi Temple of Tython]]<ref name="CL 2016" /> or the [[First Temple]] on Ossus.<ref name="Dooku: Jedi Lost" /> The Jedi maintained a connection to Tython, and one of their sacred [[Book|texts]] described "notable beasts of Tython."<ref name="TROS VD" /> Ossus was also the location of the [[Great Jedi Library]], which was destroyed before the [[Clone Wars]]<ref name="CL 2016" /> and in complete ruins by the time of its rediscovery by [[Luke Skywalker]]. Despite this, Skywalker still located an original copy of the ''[[Rammahgon]]'', one of the sacred Jedi texts, on the planet.<ref name="TROS VD" /> During the [[New Republic Era]], the [[Jedi Temple of Luke Skywalker]] was constructed on<ref name="From the Desert Comes a Stranger">{{BOBF|Chapter 6: From the Desert Comes a Stranger}}</ref> Ossus<ref name="Shadow of the Sith">''[[Shadow of the Sith]]''</ref> with the goal of becoming a "great school" for future generations of students in the restored Jedi Order.<ref name="From the Desert Comes a Stranger" />

===General perception===
====Different traditions over time====
[[File:EmerickAndKeeveHug-THRTrailOfShadows3.png|thumb|200px|Compassion and non-attachment were regarded as essential to members of the Jedi Order]]
With the galaxy changing as new threats emerged, the Jedi Order took on many forms and traditions over its life<ref name="Star Wars (2020) 20" /> in its mission to fight those threats.<ref name="Episode III" /> As such, the particular way members of the Jedi Order interpreted the current form of its ideals impacted the way they interacted with the galactic population.<ref name="Dangerous Debt">{{TCW|Dangerous Debt}}</ref> The High Republic Era was a time of greatly expanded Jedi activity, and the Order had many outposts throughout the galaxy.<ref name="The Rise of Kylo Ren 2">[[The Rise of Kylo Ren 2|''The Rise of Kylo Ren'' 2]]</ref> The Galactic Republic relied heavily on their role as guardians of peace and justice, to settle disputes through peaceful ways, to protect planetary leaders and senators.<ref name="Episode I" /> With the Republic's rapid expansion and the Order's role in protecting that effort, the Jedi also established many new temples across the Outer Rim, keeping the buildings in line with the planetary cultures they found themselves in.<ref name="Light of the Jedi" /> With many Jedi living amongst these communities<ref name="Yoda interview">{{SW|url=news/swca-2022-marvel-yoda-interview|text=SWCA 2022: Marvel's ''Star Wars: Yoda'' Miniseries Revealed}}</ref> through such temples<ref name="Light of the Jedi" /> or while out on their mission, the Jedi learned local customs<ref name="Yoda 1">[[Yoda 1|''Yoda'' 1]]</ref> and truly became part of the cultures<ref name="Yoda interview" /> they protected.<ref name="Yoda 1" />

By the time of the late Republic Era, the Order found itself shifting its focus more onto the galactic scale of the responsibilities required of the Order. In this era, the Jedi were more likely to focus on and spend their time on the galactic astropolitical scene and the bigger questions: stopping menaces, some before they even developed, and saving whole star systems at a time. Knowing that their decisions affected billions of lives, the Jedi Council regularly sent out Knights to report on the state of the various parts of the galaxy. However, as their duties amounted, several Jedi outposts were shut down to direct resources where the Republic needed the Jedi the most, and their withdrawal caused many people to abandon the settlements grown around the presence of the Order.<ref name="TLF">''[[The Living Force]]''</ref>

While [[Jedi Sentinel]]s still existed and made it their duty to work with the everyday people of the galaxy,<ref name="Endless"/> Jedi Knights found it increasingly difficult to find time for personal interaction with the people of the galaxy, with many young Jedi often forgetting that concerns that seemed small on a galactic level were often huge for the individual. That was despite Master Yoda and other Council members' efforts to teach them to pay attention to the present and where they are. In effect,<ref name="TLF" /> the Jedi Order grew increasingly isolated from the galaxy at large, much to the concern of Jedi Master Qui-Gon Jinn, due to its shrinking number of outposts.<ref name="Living Force Reveal">{{WebCite|url=https://gizmodo.com/star-wars-the-living-force-book-reveal-jedi-council-1850810565|text=The Jedi Face a Crisis in New ''Star Wars'' Novel ''The Living Force''|author=Whitbrook, James|date=2023-09-07|work=[[Wikipedia:Gizmodo|''Gizmodo'']]|archivedate=20230907173013}}</ref>

Focusing on galactic issues instead of remaining on a smaller scale, the Jedi thus were focused more and more on the future of the Republic. Secluded from the galaxy at large due to its headquarters on Coruscant,<ref name="Living Force Reveal" /> the Jedi Order was left adrift, not only because of its isolation but also how it had become heedless of strange changes within the Force.<ref name="Jedi Temple DB">{{Databank|jedi-temple|Jedi Temple}}</ref> While the Jedi still cared about the galaxy,<ref name="TLF" /> the Jedi, compared to what they were before, stayed locked away<ref name="Yoda interview" /> in their shrinking number of temples,<ref name="TLF" /> instead of remaining a part of local communities as they had in the High Republic Era.<ref name="Yoda interview" /> Jinn and Master Yoda feared that, since many people of the galaxy saw and interacted with Jedi Knights less and less, they might start to believe or could be made to believe that the Order ceased to care about them. What with many of the Order's other outposts being shut down while the Temple on Coruscant remained,<ref name="TLF" /> Jinn pondered if the Order's headquarters sitting on Coruscant was a problem, reasoning it made them look like Republic warriors. While Yoda warned him that the Council would not agree with his concerns about Coruscant, Jinn even feared the Jedi themselves could fall into that viewpoint and lose the true meaning of what it meant to be a Jedi.<ref name="Age Qui-Gon" />

During the High Republic Era, the Order and the Jedi Council enabled Jedi who were drawn to solitary action to become "[[Wayseeker]]s"—Jedi who operated outside of the dictates of the Council<ref name="Into the Dark">''[[The High Republic: Into the Dark]]''</ref> to instead follow the Force on their own terms.<ref name="THR 7">[[The High Republic (2021) 7|''The High Republic'' (2021) 7]]</ref> Such a position was seemingly not permitted in the late Republic Era due to the defiance it showed towards the council.<ref name="Padawan Novel">[[Padawan (novel)|''Padawan'']]</ref> Wayseekers were still bound to the values of the Order, so their abilities and skills could not be used in the pursuit of selfish concerns.<ref name="Into the Dark" />

Although they were not eager to be [[soldier]]s,<ref name="Episode II" /> the Jedi many times found that other skills were not enough and so their role was to fight, indeed this was understood in the High Republic as a vital, though unfortunate role of a Jedi. They took military actions against the Sith,<ref name="Light of the Jedi" /> lead soldiers against the Nihil,<ref name="The High Republic: Tempest Runner">''[[The High Republic: Tempest Runner]]''</ref> and in the last years of the Republic reprised this office, became [[general]]s of the [[Republic Military]] and fighting in the Clone Wars.<ref name="Episode III" /> Even after the fall of the Order, Jedi like Luke Skywalker and Rey fought the darkness as the edge of the powers that opposed tyranny.<ref name="Episode V" /><ref name="TROS VD" />

====Letting go====
{{Quote|Strong passions are something we try to control within ourselves, as emotions can cloud our judgment, especially in stressful situations. They can blind us to the truth, and to the leading of the Force. That said, it would be foolish to suggest that a Jedi has no desires or interests. In fact, I would go so far as to say that it would be dangerous, leading only to complacency. Yes, I have a passion for learning and teaching. It is part of who I am. But I am also prepared to set such things aside at a moment's notice. My 'passions' must never be greater than my mission.|Master Stellan Gios|<ref name="The Rising Storm" />}}
[[File:FauxFuneral-Deception.png|thumb|left|300px|To be a Jedi was to let go: friends would live and pass on, but a Jedi could not allow their connection to give way to uncontrolled grief.]]
Jedi Knights of all eras adhered to the Jedi Code, however, there were debates on how to follow its doctrines,<ref name="Master & Apprentice" /> and many Jedi developed their own personal understanding of them. One major concept was that of avoiding attachments, but different Jedi expressed different understandings of that rule.<ref name="Midnight Horizon">''[[The High Republic: Midnight Horizon]]''</ref> [[Padawan]] [[Kevmo Zink]] explained that the overall idea behind avoiding attachments was inspired by the Jedi Order's mission to the Force; if a Jedi was truly expected to work for the good of the entire galaxy, they could not tie themself down with one attachment, such as a romantic relationship with another being.<ref name="Path of Deceit">''[[The High Republic: Path of Deceit]]''</ref>

If a Jedi did form such an attachment, the Order believed that the Jedi would not be as ready to spread the ideals of the light side across the galaxy because of the relationship, which the Order taught would be a distraction. It was hoped a Jedi Knight would only be bound to the Force and the Jedi Temple.<ref name="Path of Deceit" /> High Republic Era Jedi Master [[Stellan Gios]] explained that the Jedi tried to control their strong passions because the emotions one felt for those passions could cloud their judgement, particularly during a stressful moment. Still, he said it was wrong to deny that Jedi like himself had their own interests and desires; the task of a Jedi, he reasoned, was to not let those passions take control of their being.<ref name="The Rising Storm" /> As late Republic Era [[Rael Averross]] reasoned, the text of the [[Jedi Code]] forbid strong attachments that divided one's loyalties and compromised their emotions. As such, he further reasoned that a casual physical relationship—which he enjoyed with a woman named [[Selbie]]—was only breaking the "letter" of the Jedi Code, not its actual binding spirit. While Averross's actions shocked his fellow Jedi Master [[Qui-Gon Jinn]], Averross defended himself and pointed out how Jinn had made an actual violation of the Jedi Code in his past via a relationship with an [[Unidentified individual|individual]], much to Jinn's pain. Jinn himself maintained fond memories of that past relationship despite having moved on to continue his life as a Jedi.<ref name="Master Apprentice">''[[Master & Apprentice]]''</ref>

During the High Republic Era, according to Master [[Kantam Sy]]'s viewpoint, there were many Jedi who twisted the notion non-attachment; to Sy, those who spoke of suppressing emotions, forsaking love and trying to numb themselves to discomfort were twisting Jedi teachings. Jedi like Sy and Grand Master [[Yoda]] taught non-attachment or detachment, just like love, was to let go of something or someone when the time called for it, instead of allowing oneself to be ruled by the need to possess.<ref name="Midnight Horizon" /> Indeed, close friendships were respected and valued throughout the Jedi Order,<ref name="Episode III" /> but a Jedi needed to understand they could lose those they cared for and thus would need to let go,<ref name="Midnight Horizon" /> instead of letting their grief consume them.<ref name="Episode III" />

That belief tied into the Jedi Order's beliefs on death; according to the Jedi, an individual became one with the Force upon death because they transferred into the [[Cosmic Force]], which, in turn, generated the [[Living Force]]. As High Republic Era Jedi Master [[Obratuk Glii]] explained, he was still allowed to miss his former apprentices, but he knew they were still with him through the Force, generating a joy within him that his fellows had become part of the Force. Furthermore, he declared it meant he could not be scared of his own mortality.<ref name="HRA 7">[[The High Republic Adventures (2021) 7|''The High Republic Adventures'' (2021) 7]]</ref> As selfless defenders of peace and justice,<ref name="Episode III" /> Jedi were also willing to lay down their own lives in defense of others<ref name="Supply Lines">{{TCW|Supply Lines}}</ref> and did not fear death. Instead, a Jedi was supposed to always put the needs of other people before their own needs.<ref name="Path of Vengeance" /> While Glii's commentary argued that Jedi could grieve to a certain degree so long as it did not consume them,<ref name="HRA 7" /> Padawan Matthea Cathley, who also lived during the High Republic Era, once thought to herself that Jedi did not grieve, but her thought was based in the understanding of not losing oneself to attachment over a deceased individual. Even then, she noted not grieving was hard in practice. Cathley also once claimed that Jedi did not get scared, but she later accepted that, like everyone, Jedi did indeed have fears and they needed to face them; as she put it, there was no shame in admitting when she was scared, but it was her duty to face whatever spawned that fear.<ref name="Path of Vengeance" />

[[File:YodaCouncilsAnakin-ROTS.png|thumb|right|300px|The Jedi Order taught its members to move beyond what they feared to lose, ensuring a Jedi did not cling onto the present in an ever-changing galaxy.]]
As such, a Jedi needed to be careful with their relationships<ref name="Path of Deceit" /> so as to not let attachment, and the naturally ensuing fear of losing those attachments, consume them.<ref name="Episode III" /> It was not that a Jedi was not allowed to feel romantic love, as it was understood such feelings were natural, but a Jedi were not supposed to enter into a romantic relationship. It was essential for a Jedi Knight to make the right choice for the Order and not neglect their Jedi duties in the favor of their beloved, even if that would mean the end of the relationship.<ref name="The Rise of Clovis">{{TCW|The Rise of Clovis}}</ref><ref name="VoT" /> As Zink explained, a Jedi was able to use the Force to direct themselves through the tides of strong emotions and learn what the underlining motivations of those feelings were. With that, a Jedi would not be ruled by their passions, but instead could make tactical decisions with a better understanding of their own mindset.<ref name="Path of Deceit" />

For some outsiders, as such, the Jedi teaching on the matter often misinterpreted, believed the Jedi to be distant and seemingly unconcerned about the lives of those they were to protect<ref name="Dangerous Debt" /> and that "love" was forbidden for a Jedi.<ref name="Episode II" /> The actions of some emotionally distant Jedi Knights did little to help that misconception:<ref name="Dangerous Debt" /><ref name="Convergence" /> during the days of the High Republic, a Jedi Knight saved the lives of a starship crew, including the young [[Axel Greylark]], but needed to drag the badly injured boy away from [[Lexxir Greylark|his father]], trapped under the rubble, thus leaving him behind. The Jedi told him that what happened was the will of the Force, something that left Greylark baffled. Years later, when he recounted the incident to Jedi Knight [[Gella Nattai]] and how the Knight later forgot his face, he concluded that one grieving boy in a galaxy full of them was nothing, but Nattai affirmed to Greylark that one grieving boy was everything.<ref name="Convergence" />

Jedi of the High Republic Era displayed a great deal of emotional openness to those grieving<ref name="THR (2021) 3">[[The High Republic (2021) 3|''The High Republic'' (2021) 3]]</ref> or upset<ref name="Trail of Shadows 3">[[The High Republic: Trail of Shadows 3|''The High Republic: Trail of Shadows'' 3]]</ref>—showing it in actions like offering a hug.<ref name="THR (2021) 3" /><ref name="Trail of Shadows 3" /> During the waning decades of the Republic, compassion was still meant to be central to a Jedi's life,<ref name="Episode II" /> with the non-attachment rule defined in theory as someone not meant to fearfully grasp on things and beings in a [[universe]] ruled by change.<ref name="Episode II" /><ref name="The Rise of Clovis" /><ref name="Kanan 1">[[Kanan 1|''Kanan'' 1]]</ref><ref name="Jedi Crash">{{TCW|Jedi Crash}}</ref><ref name="Episode III" /> However, the actions of a Jedi appearing not to care could damage the entire Order's reputation<ref name="Dangerous Debt" /> or result in that Jedi failing to help someone who needed support. Jedi Knight [[Thirteenth Sister|Iskat Akaris]] found a lack of support amongst the Jedi as she grappled with her insecurities during the late Republic Era.<ref name="Rise of the Red Blade">''[[Inquisitor: Rise of the Red Blade]]''</ref> 

During the Clone Wars, amid an [[Senate hostage crisis|incident]] on Coruscant in which the Jedi attempted to recapture the [[Hutt]] [[crime lord]] [[Ziro Desilijic Tiure]], a Jedi was forced to redirect a transport to ensure it avoided hitting a platform of people, but the redirected ship instead crashed into the [[Martez family]] apartment. Two children—[[Trace Martez|Trace]] and [[Rafa Martez]]—became orphans in the incident with the demise of their parents in the crash. The Jedi in question then failed to properly explain herself in a compassionate matter, trying to assure both that she had needed to make a choice but that the Force would still be with them, a phrase that meant nothing to the two girls.<ref name="Dangerous Debt" />

[[File:LukeYodasSecondLightsaber-FromTheDesertComesAStranger.png|thumb|left|250px|For Luke Skywalker, surrendering to one's attachments was to forsake the ways of the Jedi Order.]]
Luke Skywalker affirmed the Jedi Order's commitment to non-attachment since the early days of his efforts to rebuild it.<ref name="From the Desert Comes a Stranger" /> However, he also told his sister, [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Organa]], that she could still be [[Marriage|married]] to [[Han Solo]] if she joined the Order.<ref name="The Princess and the Scoundrel">''[[The Princess and the Scoundrel]]''</ref> Due to his unorthodox training in the Jedi arts, Skywalker sought to provide the next generation of Jedi with a more conventional path to knighthood.<ref name="Secrets of the Jedi" /> As the last Jedi of his era,<ref name="Rise of Kylo Ren 2" /> Skywalker found comfort through adherence to the Jedi way, having largely isolated himself from his friends while rebuilding the Order.<ref name="Shadow of the Sith" /> In addition, [[the Armorer]]—a [[Mandalorian]] who was familiar with Jedi history—stated that the Jedi were required to sacrifice personal attachments in order to master the ways of the Force.<ref name="Chapter 5: Return of the Mandalorian">{{BOBF|Chapter 5: Return of the Mandalorian}}</ref> While in the process of training [[Din Grogu|Grogu]], Skywalker informed his apprentice that giving into attachments to those whom he loved was to forsake the way of the Jedi.<ref name="From the Desert Comes a Stranger" />

====Views on the lightsaber====
[[File:Indara-JSW.png|thumb|200px|right|Indara chose to only draw her lightsaber as a last resort.]]
{{Quote|I realized something. Something important. A Jedi isn't his lightsaber. A lightsaber is just a tool. A Jedi is someone who uses the Force to bring light to the galaxy. To protect people and push back darkness. That particular lightsaber… the one I lost… I don't need it. It was my father's… but I'm not sure how I feel about that anymore. I'm going to be a Jedi. Even if I never see that lightsaber again.|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="SW 2020 4">[[Star Wars (2020) 4|''Star Wars'' (2020) 4]]</ref>}}
During the history of the Jedi Order, the lightsaber considered to be the Jedi's only true ally in battle.<ref name="Test of Strength">{{TCW|A Test of Strength}}</ref> Beyond its existence as a weapon,<ref name="Episode II" /> however, the lightsaber was a symbol of care to the Jedi; as explained by Jedi Knight Obi-Wan Kenobi, the blade represented the care with which they approached and wielded their gifts, further serving as a message to their opponents and representing something that needed great skill and training to wield. In choosing a rather limited weapon, Kenobi explained, the lightsaber was also the symbol of their role and duty as protectors, not destroyers. Further reasoned by Kenobi to be a tool that showed the Jedi could be more powerful but openly chose not to be, the lightsaber represented a Jedi's duty to stand tall against darkness.<ref name="The Lesson Anakin">{{StoryCite|story=The Lesson (Obi-Wan & Anakin)|book=Star Wars (2020) 25|sformatted=The Lesson (Obi-Wan & Anakin)}}</ref> While the phrase "this weapon is your life" was popular during the late Republic Era,<ref name="Episode II" /><ref name="Hostage Crisis">{{TCW|Hostage Crisis}}</ref> a Jedi—as exemplified by the High Republic Era<ref name="Cataclysm">''[[The High Republic: Cataclysm]]''</ref> and Kenobi's speech<ref name="The Lesson Anakin" />—needed to be more than their lightsaber.<ref name="Cataclysm" /> Indeed, after the Order's fall, Luke Skywalker came to realize that a Jedi was not their lightsaber and instead the duty he was tasked with was something beyond a single weapon. Faced with either needing to rescue his friend [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Organa]] or continue looking for his lightsaber on [[Cloud City]], Skywalker came to his decision and left to help his friend.<ref name="SW 2020 4" />

[[File:KhofarMissionJedi-OfficialAcolyteX.png|thumb|left|275px|The High Republic Era saw a greater variance in lightsaber color than just green and blue lightsabers.]]
The High Republic Era would also see a greater variance in lightsaber color<ref name="THR 8">[[The High Republic (2021) 8|''The High Republic'' (2021) 8]]</ref><ref name="Trailer">{{SWYouTube|BtytYWhg2mc|The Acolyte | Official Trailer | Disney+}}</ref> and hilt design<ref name="Shadows 1">[[The High Republic – Shadows of Starlight 1|''The High Republic – Shadows of Starlight'' 1]]</ref> than the Jedi of the late Republic. During the late Republic era, Jedi largely wielded blue or green lightsabers with simple silver hilts.<ref name="Episode II" /> A shift in lightsaber design could be seen in the High Republic Era: the [[Nihil conflict]] saw the Jedi Order implement the [[Guardian Protocols]] and prepare for greater conflict, during which the High Council knew Jedi could lose their blade and be forced to quickly replace it. Rather than have Jedi spend time building elaborated hilts, the Council reasoned that speed and effectiveness was of the essence and that a ready supply of lightsabers was something the Order needed. As such, the Council ordered Jedi to only construct [[battle saber]]s, hilts with a simple design<ref name="Shadows 1" /> like those seen in the late Republic Era.<ref name="Episode II" /> Even then, Jedi active in the later High Republic Era still had a wider variance in lightsaber color<ref name="Trailer" /> than the blue and green-filled Order of the late Republic.<ref name="Episode II" /> While each individual Jedi built their own lightsaber as a youngling to their own specifications, allowing for a degree of individuality in hilt design even during the late Republic Era,<ref name="Test of Strength" /> groupings of Jedi of that time were largely defined by cylindrical silver hilts of an overall similar design, with those hilts mainly producing green or blue blades.<ref name="Episode II" />

====Who gets to join====
{{Quote|Wait— you mean, I can be a Jedi?''"<br />"''Study you should, the Farseeker Lyr. No great power had he, yet from his ink sprang some of the Jedi's greatest texts. For though the Jedi and the Force are one, the Force is not what a Jedi makes.''"<br />"''Then what makes a Jedi?''"<br />"''That is something only you can answer.|Lohim Nara and Yoda during the High Republic Era|<ref name="What a Jedi Makes">{{StoryCite|story=What a Jedi Makes|book=Stories of Jedi and Sith}}</ref>}}
During the High Republic Era, when the young boy [[Lohim Nara]] spoke to Grand Master Yoda about his life-long desire to join the Order, he confessed that he had faked his Force-sensitivity and midi-chlorian count, but Yoda told the boy of how the Force was not the sole answer to what a Jedi was. Yoda spoke of how the Force existed in all life forms and how, if he wanted to be someone worthy of the name Jedi, he could not dwell on what he did not have. He also told the boy not to worry about being "too old" to become a youngling, stating that he was still learning at over six hundred years old. While Yoda seemed to admit the boy could not be a Jedi Knight due to his age, he noted that other professions helped the Order and offered him a role as a [[guardian of the Temple]] after Nara realized he had a mentor in [[Reina Bilass]].<ref name="What a Jedi Makes" />

[[File:Kenobi_and_Yoda_Turret_Room.png|thumb|right|300px|Yoda served as a Jedi Master for centuries and thus was present to watch as the Jedi Order changed with the galaxy.]]
However, later in its history, the Order proved reluctant but willing to bend their rules as the death of Jedi Master [[Qui-Gon Jinn]] and return of Sith convinced the Council to allow the young Anakin Skywalker to be trained, despite he was initially deemed too old.<ref name="Episode I" /> Decades later, Yoda also chose to take Luke Skywalker as his apprentice after initially pointing out how old he was.<ref name="Episode V" /> Alternatively, the Order of the High Republic also became a home for the young Force-sensitive [[Zeen Mrala-Talisola|Zeen Mrala]], who did not train as a Jedi but became close to many Padawans and, despite Jedi regulations forbidding its members from engaging in romance, fell in love with Padawan [[Lula Mrala-Talisola|Lula Talisola]].<ref name="Midnight Horizon" /> Despite not being a Jedi, she was welcomed to train with the Padawans and their masters, with Talisola's master Kantam Sy helping her to control her anger.<ref name="THRA 12">[[The High Republic Adventures (2021) 12|''The High Republic Adventures'' (2021) 12]]</ref> Later, in order to find and save Talisola, Mrala realized that she was willing to join the ranks of the Jedi despite her age, although her realization came from how she figured certain Jedi rules would be bent due to the evolving Nihil conflict.<ref name="Midnight Horizon" />

The High Republic Era Order also maintained close ties with other Force orders, working as part of the [[Convocation of the Force]] on Jedha.<ref name="Path of Deceit" /> Through its ties to other orders, the Jedi brought rescued children who were "too old" to be trained as Jedi to Jedha, where they were taken in by other faiths.<ref name="Convergence" /> In the late Republic Era, the Jedi continued to help orphans find new homes, but there were instances where they failed to do so because of local law<ref name="The Weapon">{{StoryCite|story=The Weapon|book=Age of Republic Special 1}}</ref> or their own non-attachment rule going so far it blurred the line between non-attachment and apathy, such as when a Jedi of the late Republic Era left Trace and Rafa Martez on their own after the deaths of their parents. Beyond those deaths being an indirect result of the Jedi's own actions, the Jedi's apparent lack of concern and the fact they simply left the two girls on their own created a distaste in both girls towards the Jedi Order, on whom they did not want to depend upon.<ref name="Dangerous Debt" />

====Leaving the Order====
{{Quote|The Jedi have been my family since I was a child, the Temple my home. But my future lies here, on Serenno ''[…]'' I am the heir of Serenno. I will rebuild this planet, make it stronger. Not for the Republic. And not for the Jedi. But for the people. My people. I ask for you, and the Council, to respect my decision in this matter.''"<br />"''Hm. Saddened by your decision we are, but honor it we will.''"<br />"''Thank you. I will surrender my lightsaber to Master Kostana.''"<br />"''No. Necessary that will not be.''"<br />"''It is the weapon of a Jedi.''"<br />"''Which is why keep it you must. More than a name, a Jedi is. More than a title. Strong in the Force, you are. Guide you, it will. Guide us all, it must.|Dooku speaks to Yoda about his decision|<ref name="Dooku: Jedi Lost">''[[Dooku: Jedi Lost]]''</ref>}}
While Grand Master Yoda once declared in the late Republic Era that a Jedi leaving the Order was a failure on the Order's part to keep that Jedi where they belonged, Jedi were permitted to leave the Jedi Order whenever they so chose without punishment,<ref name="Cataclysm" /> with Yoda accepting that fact, even if a Jedi leaving might sadden him. Should they feel it to be their calling, a former Jedi was even permitted to return to the Order. During the High Republic Era, one of Yoda's Padawans, Kantam Sy, walked away from the Jedi for over a year before returning when they met their soon-to-be Padawan [[Lula Mrala-Talisola|Lula Talisola]], who made Sy believe their place was with the Order. Jedi Master [[Cohmac Vitus]] was surprised Yoda would allow his Padawan to do a walkaway from the Order, but Sy believed Yoda knew it was necessary for them to find their place in the Order, with Sy at the time also thinking Yoda had seen it coming and thus was why he had held off on their Knighting.<ref name="Midnight Horizon" />

Twenty Jedi Masters who departed the Order became known as the "[[Lost Twenty]]" and were commemorated by busts located in the [[Jedi Archives]] of the Temple. Yoda believed the Lost needed to be remembered and honored, which enabled the Jedi to learn from the failings that caused them to depart. Still, he advised the Jedi not to live in the past just because prior members had departed. The twentieth of the Lost was [[Dooku]],<ref name="Absolutely Everything">''[[Star Wars: Absolutely Everything You Need to Know]]''</ref> a former Padawan of Yoda who departed in [[42 BBY]] over issues with the Jedi and Republic. Despite his departure, Dooku was allowed to visit the Temple on Coruscant,<ref name="Padawan novel">[[Padawan (novel)|''Padawan'']]</ref> continued to have access to its Archives,<ref name="The Sith Lord">{{TOTJ|The Sith Lord}}</ref> and was even allowed to retain possession of his lightsaber, with Yoda reasoning being a Jedi was more than a title and thus the weapon would still be of use to him.<ref name="Dooku: Jedi Lost" />

====Public image====
=====Jedi unite for peace=====
{{Quote|The ''Jedi Order'' was strong, deeply seated into the galaxy's consciousness. They had a great temple in the heart of ''Coruscant'', worked closely with ''the Republic'' and its chancellor for centuries. People made holodramas about them, wrote stories. The Jedi were…''celebrities'', and rightly so.|The Archivist|<ref name="Crimson Reign 3">[[Crimson Reign 3|''Crimson Reign'' 3]]</ref>}}
[[File:Jedi_Unite_for_Peace.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Jedi were committed in their duty as guardians of the Republic, but had no desire to be celebrated as such.]]
The Jedi Order was regarded as a mysterious organization throughout its history.<ref name="Propaganda">''[[Star Wars Propaganda: A History of Persuasive Art in the Galaxy]]''</ref> The Order was ingrained in the public consciousness of the galaxy as a result of its strong ties to the government of the Galactic Republic,<ref name="Crimson Reign 3" /> but was nonetheless inscrutable to the general public. However, those who encountered the Jedi were awed by their Force abilities.<ref name="Propaganda" /> Nevertheless, the Jedi were celebrities of their time, a status reflected by Jedi-themed [[holodrama]]s as well as stories written about the Order. [[The Archivist]], a [[historian]] who studied Jedi lore prior to joining [[Crimson Dawn]], believed that to most people the Jedi exemplified the moral virtues that were affiliated with the light side of the Force, making them both loved and hated by the galaxy that they served.<ref name="Crimson Reign 3" />

During the High Republic, the Jedi actively worked to be part of local communities<ref name="Yoda interview" /> in addition to helping them.<ref name="Yoda 1" /> However, the [[Path of the Open Hand]] cult spread its influence by claiming all who used the Force were violating the energy field from its freedom, claiming that each use of the Force to solve a problem created an equal or worse problem elsewhere.<ref name="Perspective">{{InsiderCite|215|Tales of Enlightenment: A Different Perspective}}</ref> The people of the planet [[E'ronoh]] were divided on the Jedi, with some believing they were miracle workers while others looked down on the Order as a group of Republic-backed outsiders. The arrival of the Path galvanized anti-Jedi sentiment on E'ronoh.<ref name="Convergence" /> The Path would, in time, evolve into the militant [[Path of the Closed Fist]] that hoped to war with the Jedi.<ref name="Path of Vengeance" /> Open Hand-adherent Path members<ref name="Cataclysm" /> and the Path's descendant movement,<ref name="Chronicles of the Jedi">''[[Star Wars: The High Republic: Chronicles of the Jedi]]''</ref> the [[Elders of the Path]],<ref name="HRA 1">[[The High Republic Adventures (2021) 1|''The High Republic Adventures'' (2021) 1]]</ref> maintained their [[anti-Jedi sentiment]].<ref name="Cataclysm" /><ref name="HRA 1" /> Open Hand [[Elder (Path of the Open Hand)|Elder]] [[Yulon Onning]] abandoned the Closed Fist yet still hoped to see it triumph over the Jedi, whom he continued to see as "filth" and a group whose defeat would be a victory for the Open Hand.<ref name="Cataclysm" /> In the end, the Closed Fist would be succeeded by the [[Nihil]],<ref name="Path of Vengeance" /> a group of marauders who waged war with the Jedi and Republic in their name of securing what they saw as their true freedom.<ref name="Light of the Jedi" />

In most situations, the Jedi were meant to lead rebuilding efforts after a battle had brought damage. However, the Holy Moon of Jedha presented an interesting case after the [[Battle of Jedha]]:<ref name="Path of Vengeance" /> in the past, the Jedi had once governed—and, in the opinion of some, "ruled over"—Jedha City<ref name="THR 2022 1">[[The High Republic (2022) 1|''The High Republic'' (2022) 1]]</ref> until the other religions who considered Jedha holy objected, believing that the Jedi were trying to depict themselves as the one true faith. The Jedi agreed to step down from their control,<ref name="Path of Vengeance" /> but lingering [[anti-Jedi sentiment]] remained,<ref name="THR 2022 1" /> with one Jedha citizen once accusing the Jedi of always trying to control their lives.<ref name="THR 2022 5">[[The High Republic (2022) 5|''The High Republic'' (2022) 5]]</ref> That lingering anti-Jedi sentiment was used by the Path of the Open Hand to help bring the Battle of Jedha into being<ref name="Path of Vengeance" /> in [[382 BBY]].<ref name="Timelines" /> After the battle, it was decided the [[Guardians of the Whills]] would serve as a steadying hand in the rebuilding effort instead of the Jedi because of still-lingering mistrust towards the Order on the part of Jedha citizens.<ref name="Path of Vengeance" />

Public discontent with the Galactic Senate led to manifestations of anti-Jedi sentiment during the late Republic Era.<ref name="Choices">{{TOTJ|Choices}}</ref> As an extension of the Galactic Republic's [[Judicial Department]], the Jedi Order was subject to the authority of the Senate,<ref name="TSWB" /> and therefore, considered an instrument of the government's will. This belief was held both within the Senate<ref name="Justice">{{TOTJ|Justice (episode)}}</ref> and without;<ref name="Choices" /> Senator [[Dagonet]] said that the Jedi served the Senate,<ref name="Justice" /> and [[Semage]] of [[Raxus Secundus]] accused them of being "lapdogs" for the Republic government. Hostility toward the Jedi extended to violent acts against members of the Order, such as Jedi Master [[Katri]] who was [[Ambush on Raxus Secundus|killed]] by Semage and his guards.<ref name="Choices" /> Jedi Master Dooku became disenchanted with both the Senate and the Order, claiming that the Jedi served a "corrupt Senate" that had "failed the Republic"<ref name="The Sith Lord" /> in contrast to his former belief that the Jedi were servants of the Republic and its people.<ref name="Justice" />

=====Clone War anti-Jedi views=====
======Harmful propaganda======
{{Quote|Emperor ''Palpatine'' rose to power because he made people afraid. He made people fear the ''Jedi''. He called them ''traitors''. He said they used their mystical powers to subvert democracy and peace, to cause war. It worked, possibly because as much as people ''loved'' the Jedi, they also ''hated'' them, on some level. It is hard to look at people who have become their ''best selves''. It reminds you that ''you have not''.|The Archivist|<ref name="Crimson Reign 3" />}}
{{Expandsect}}
During the Clone Wars, the [[Commission for the Protection of the Republic]] (COMPOR) initially attempted to use the Order as an [[Jedi Unite for Peace|instrument]] for wartime [[propaganda]]. This idea was discouraged by Supreme Chancellor Sheev Palpatine, who noted the Jedi's discomfort with their new role as generals in the Republic Military. COMPOR therefore focused its propaganda on the [[clone trooper]]s who served under the Jedi's command, and as such the Jedi's contributions to the Republic war effort went largely unnoticed.<ref name="Propaganda" />

[[File:Deliver_Us_From_Jedi_Evil.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Anti-Jedi sentiment grew as a result of the Order's involvement in the Clone Wars.]]
The Jedi Order was vilified during the Clone Wars by elements within both the Republic and the Confederacy. The Separatists<ref name="Propaganda" />—while, in total truth, being proponents of slavery at the secret direction of [[Count]] [[Dooku]]<ref name="Kidnapped" />—accused the Jedi of hypocrisy, claiming that the Order supported the Republic's inhumane practice—as portrayed through Separatist propaganda—of using clones as a "[[Slavery|slave]] [[army]]." The Separatists' portrayal of the Jedi largely succeeded in maligning their character in the eyes of the public, as few in the Confederacy knew that the Order was not given a choice in the matter. It was the Order's preference to not be used as icons of heroism, having long resisted attempts at romanticizing the Jedi, especially for the purpose of wartime propaganda. The Jedi, therefore, were mostly absent from galactic culture, which allowed anti-Jedi sentiment to grow from preconceptions instead of a genuine rebuttal of the Order's practices. The Separatists were convinced that the Order neglected their worlds, forcing them to secede from the Republic. Separatist civilians conflated the Order with the [[Core Worlds]]' reputation for cultural elitism, and some even viewed the Jedi as "[[Deliver Us From Jedi Evil|evil]]" and a fanatical sect of "warmongers." The historian [[Janyor]] theorized that such misconceptions of the Jedi could have been averted if the Order had been more engaged with the populace.<ref name="Propaganda" />

Anti-Jedi sentiment was openly espoused by the Republic's successor, the [[Galactic Empire]].<ref name="Propaganda" /> The self-proclaimed [[Galactic Emperor]] Sheev Palpatine deceitfully accused the entire Order of [[treason]], maligning the Order's reputation<ref name="Catalyst" /> after several members of the High Council, having discovered his true identity as the Sith Lord, Darth Sidious, attempted to [[Duel in Palpatine's office|arrest the chancellor]].<ref name="Episode III" /> Palpatine squarely blamed the Jedi for recent troubles<ref name="Launchpad229">{{InsiderCite|229|Launchpad}}</ref> and deceitfully accused them of using their "mystical powers" to cause the Clone War<ref name="Crimson Reign 3" /> in order to secure power. [[Grand Vizier]] [[Mas Amedda]] publicly [[Celebration marking the eradication of the Jedi Order|denounced]] the Order as a symbol of fear and referred to the Order's downfall as the beginning of a new "age of freedom."<ref name="Dark Lord 1">[[Darth Vader (2017) 1|''Darth Vader'' (2017) 1]]</ref> With the exception of individuals like Lyra Erso, the Order was effectively forgotten by the galaxy during the early days of the [[Imperial Era]]<ref name="Catalyst" /> as the Empire quickly moved to cover up its existence.<ref name="Crimson Reign 3" /> Despite the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic]] largely having pro-Jedi views,<ref name="Star Wars (2020) 26">[[Star Wars (2020) 26|''Star Wars'' (2020) 26]]</ref> its individual members were not immune to the era's bias, with soldier [[Reyé Hollis]] believing stories he heard of the Jedi being ineffective fools who stole glory for themselves.<ref name="Luke on the Bright Side">{{StoryCite|story=Luke on the Bright Side|book=Stories of Jedi and Sith}}</ref>

======Collapse in public opinion======
{{Quote|Aren't you a bit busy with your war to be bothering with the likes of us?|[[Tiggs Leo]]|<ref name="Lethal Trackdown">{{TCW|Lethal Trackdown}}</ref>}}
In general, Republic citizens were unaware of the Order's motives for fighting in the Clone Wars. To some individuals,<ref name="Propaganda" /> such as the Mandalorian [[Duchess]] [[Satine Kryze]]<ref name="VoT">{{TCW|Voyage of Temptation}}</ref> or the [[Coruscanti]] civilian [[Letta Turmond]], the Jedi had forsaken their principles as peacekeepers by becoming military leaders and battlefield warriors.<ref name="The Jedi Who Knew Too Much">{{TCW|The Jedi Who Knew Too Much}}</ref> [[Tee Watt Kaa]], the head of a [[Lurmen village]] on [[Maridun]], rejected the argument that the Jedi were peacekeepers, a function that he believed was incompatible with the Order's participation in the Clone Wars.<ref name="Jedi Crash" /> A committed, to the death, pacifist, Kaa was unwilling to compromise his views, refusing to permit the other [[Lurmen]]s to defend their village against the [[Confederacy military]]. They ultimately defied Kaa's authority upon witnessing the Jedi's efforts to [[Defense of Maridun|save the Lurmens]] from [[Defoliator|extermination]] by General [[Lok Durd]].<ref name="DoP">{{TCW|Defenders of Peace}}</ref>

[[File:Tiplar_and_Doom_marines.png|thumb|right|300px|When the Jedi became military generals during the last years of the Republic, many took them as warmongers who had abandoned their peacekeeping mission.]]
At the height of the Clone Wars, people throughout the galaxy regarded the Jedi as warmongers, particularly in territories controlled by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. However, most beings did not know that the Order went to war to honor its commitment to protect the Senate, the Republic,<ref name="Propaganda" /> and the galaxy at large.<ref name="Mace Windu 5">[[Jedi of the Republic – Mace Windu 5|''Jedi of the Republic – Mace Windu'' 5]]</ref> Nevertheless, the Clone Wars caused civilians<ref name="Old Friends">{{TCW|Old Friends Not Forgotten}}</ref> like Turmond<ref name="The Jedi Who Knew Too Much" /> and the Martez sisters to lose faith in the Order.<ref name="Raffa DB">{{Databank|rafa|Rafa}}</ref> Rafa Martez and her sister, Trace Martez, faulted the Order for the deaths of their parents when a Jedi had redirected a transport from a platform of people, only for it to hit their apartment. With the Jedi seemingly uncompassionate towards their plight, the sisters found themselves left alone and with anger at the Jedi.<ref name="Dangerous Debt" />

While Separatists were opposed to the Jedi due to their allegiance with the Republic, with many of [[Lux Bonteri]]'s friends blaming the Jedi for the bloodshed of the Clone Wars,<ref name="Heroes on Both Sides">{{TCW|Heroes on Both Sides}}</ref> the citizens of the Republic would turn on the Jedi due to losing faith in their Order. To the public, the Jedi looked like a force who seemed more interested in playing politics<ref name="Old Friends" /> or waging war<ref name="Lethal Trackdown" /> than helping those in danger<ref name="Old Friends" /> or need.<ref name="Gone with a Trace">{{TCW|Gone with a Trace}}</ref> Indeed, in focusing on the Clone Wars, the Jedi had less time to aid the people of the Republic they were meant to protect.<ref name="Lethal Trackdown" /> Trace Martez thought of the Jedi as a police force who started the war and had forgotten about the less well off citizens of Coruscant.<ref name="Gone with a Trace" /> While a concern about the political ramifications of their actions could be seen in the late High Republic Era,<ref name="LostFound">{{Acolyte|Lost / Found}}</ref> former Jedi Ahsoka Tano believed that the Jedi of the Clone Wars era had overwhelmingly tied themselves to the Republic's political state, with Tano herself only regaining her faith in the Jedi when she was reminded of how important the Order could be when it helped those in need. Even then, she made sure to criticize what she regarded as political chess playing on the Order's part when again confronted by it.<ref name="Old Friends" />

=====Late Republic pro-Jedi views=====
{{Quote|But at what cost? It's like you once said. Many lives get caught between the Republic and the Separatists.''"<br />"''I did say that once, but after watching the heroics and selflessness of the Jedi, I do believe the Republic is the right side to be on.|Ahsoka Tano and Lux Bonteri|<ref name="Tipping Points">{{TCW|Tipping Points}}</ref>}}
[[File:Lyra_Erso-RO-Collectors_Edition-Titan.png|thumb|left|250px|Lyra Erso was a spiritual woman who supported the Jedi Order out of reverence of the Force.]]
Prior to the Clone Wars, the people of the Galactic Republic kept faith with the Jedi Order<ref name="AoR-Maul">[[Age of Republic - Darth Maul 1|''Age of Republic - Darth Maul'' 1]]</ref> that had acquitted itself through a thousand years of loyal service.<ref name="TSWB" /> [[Lyra Erso]], the [[Marriage|wife]] of [[Doctor]] [[Galen Walton Erso]], supported the Order's views on charity, compassion, and peace. Erso's support for the Order was rooted in her faith in the Force, which evolved from her lifelong love of nature, and her belief in the concepts of good and evil. The Force, as Erso viewed it, was the epitome of perfection and good. During the Clone Wars, Erso believed that the Order was the Republic's best defense against the "powers of evil." Erso also admired the Jedi Temple on Coruscant for its elegance and location on a Force nexus. She often exercised on the Temple grounds prior to the Clone Wars; after the conflict began, however, Erso noticed a changed atmosphere at the now heavily patrolled [[Palace Court|temple district]], which made the area virtually inaccessible to the public with the exception of individuals who carried high security clearance.<ref name="Catalyst" />

Erso's [[Reproduction|pregnancy]] was the closest she felt to experiencing the Force in the way that she imagined the Jedi's connection to it—an incomprehensible and "profound connection with life." She recognized the Force as the source of the Jedi's power, granting them abilities that far exceeded Erso's strengths and intuition. However, she comforted herself with the belief that while she could not use the Force as the Jedi could, she was able to sense it nonetheless. In the aftermath of the [[Order 66]], Erso rejected the Galactic Empire's explanation for the [[Great Jedi Purge]], refusing to believe that the Jedi betrayed the Republic. Whereas the public generally accepted the deaths of thousands of Jedi, largely out of relief over the end of the Clone Wars, Erso was heartbroken by the Order's destruction and cried for [[Standard hour|hours]] as a result, an experience she likened to the loss of a family member. Despite the eradication of the Order, Erso continued to believe in the Jedi whose principles influenced her sense of morality.<ref name="Catalyst" />

[[File:Saw_Lux_Anakin_Ahsoka_Jedi_Temple.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Though a former Separatist, Lux Bonteri grew to see the Jedi as heroes for their role in the liberation of Onderon.]]
In addition to Erso,<ref name="Catalyst" /> the Jedi's deeds won the respect and admiration of individuals who worked closely with the Order, such as [[Cham Syndulla]]<ref name="SWR:Homecoming">{{Rebels|Homecoming}}</ref> and [[Lux Bonteri]].<ref name="Tipping Points" /> During the [[Battle of Ryloth]] of the Clone War, Syndulla was initially hesitant to accept the Order's offer of an alliance between the [[Grand Army of the Republic]] and the [[Twi'lek Resistance]], knowing that the Republic would occupy [[Ryloth]] in the battle's aftermath.<ref name="Liberty on Ryloth">{{TCW|Liberty on Ryloth}}</ref> Ultimately, though, Syndulla developed a newfound respect of the Jedi for their role in the liberation of his homeworld, particularly [[Jedi General]] [[Mace Windu]], whose memory the [[Twi'lek]]s honored in songs dedicated to the liberation of their [[homeworld]].<ref name="SWR:Homecoming" /> Bonteri worked with the Order during the [[Onderonian Civil War]],<ref name="Tipping Points" /> having joined the [[Onderon rebels]] after his [[House Bonteri|mother]], [[Separatist Senate|Separatist Senator]] [[Mina Bonteri]], was [[assassin]]ated at the behest of Count Dooku.<ref name="Lux Bonteri DB">{{Databank|lux-bonteri|Lux Bonteri}}</ref> By training and fighting alongside the Onderon rebels, the Jedi changed Bonteri's views on the Republic and the Order. Inspired by the selflessness and heroism of the Order, Bonteri vowed to restore the union between [[Onderon]] and the Republic.<ref name="Tipping Points" />

==History==
===Ancient times===
{{Quote|For over a thousand generations the Jedi Knights were the guardians of peace and justice in the Old Republic. Before the dark times. Before the Empire.|Obi-Wan Kenobi|<ref name="Episode IV" />}}
[[File:Prime_Jedi.png|thumb|left|200px|The Jedi Order began with the Prime Jedi a thousand generations before the Battle of Yavin.]]
The precursors of the Jedi were the [[Dai Bendu]],<ref name="Unlimited Power">''[[Unlimited Power]]''</ref> but the Jedi Order itself was founded in the distant past on planet Ahch-To by the Prime Jedi<ref name="TLJ VD" /> in [[25,025 BBY]].<ref name="GalacticAtlasNew" /> According to the legend, [[Force-imbued sword]]s were used prior to invention of the lightsaber by the earlier Force adepts and the Jedi Order.<ref name="NoP">''[[Nexus of Power]]''</ref> At the dawn of their order, the Jedi came into possession of Ilum, an ancient planet encased in [[Water|ice]] and littered with kyber crystals, the heart of the Jedi lightsaber. Considered the Jedi Order's most sacred world, its ancient Temple would eventually host the Gathering, a tradition that would continue up until the fall of the Order.<ref name="DB:Ilum" /> As the Jedi were spread throughout the galaxy, they acquired a vessel called the ''[[Crucible]]'' that would ferry students to Ilum so that they could perform the Gathering. It would become the base of the [[architect droid]], [[Professor]] [[Huyang]], for over a thousand [[Standard year|years]] as he instructed on lightsaber designs and kept records of every single Jedi weapon created.<ref name="The Gathering" /><ref name=":1">{{TCW|A Test of Strength}}</ref> During this early period, the Order also established itself on Ossus, Jedha, [[Xenxiar]], and other worlds.<ref name="TROS JN">''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: A Junior Novel]]''</ref> Later, some Jedi [[scholar]]s argued that a number of locations could be considered the birthplace of the Order. The scholars argued that Coruscant, Jedha, and Ossus as well as worlds shrouded in myth, such as Tython and Ahch-To could all be considered candidate worlds for the Order's birth and the location of the first Jedi temple.<ref name="CL 2016" />

The Jedi religion, as it was called by Luke Skywalker,<ref name="Episode VIII" /> was based around eight "sacred texts" that "describe tenets, history, and specific guidance to those studying the path of the Jedi." These texts included the ''Rammahgon'' and the ''[[Aionomica]]''—separated into two volumes, ''Aionomicum I'' and ''Aionomicum II''. These books included a number of aspects of the Jedi religion that appeared to have little influence on later Jedi, such as references to "gods" and the [[World Between Worlds]]. They also included more mundane information, such as lightsaber designs. The theology of the Jedi religion changed over time; many Jedi, such as Master [[Odan-Urr]], annotated even the sacred texts. Much was added to the Jedi canon over time, giving rise to the belief that the ''Aionomicum II'' doubled in size during its compilation.<ref name="TROS VD" /> The fortunes of the Jedi waxed and waned over the centuries; multiple times throughout its long history, the Order had been reduced to a mere handful of adherents. <ref name="Light of the Jedi" />

At some point prior to around [[20,000 BBY]],<ref name="Ordu Date">''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' dates the defeat of the [[Ordu Aspectu]] around [[20,000 BBY]]. Therefore, Ordu Aspectu must have been active before c. 20,000 [['ABY-BBY' dating system|BBY]].</ref> a Jedi sect known as the [[Ordu Aspectu]] broke off from the Jedi Order. Led by the enigmatic [[Rur]] and his second in command, [[Amon]], they sought to achieve the secrets to immortality. Eventually, a [[Entruvia Conflict|conflict]] broke out between them and the orthodox Jedi. The conflict ended with the signing of a [[Jedi–Ordu Aspectu treaty|peace treaty]]. The treaty allowed the Ordu Aspectu to continue their research but under Jedi scrutiny.
